channel.title: Posts on Dr Jas Powell
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Dr Jas Powell

item.title: 🍴 Foccacia Recipe 🫓 🧀 👩‍🍳
item.description: I always forget the weights and measures, so this is an aide memoire for the perfect foccacia base recipe… Foccacia Weights and Measures Mix together: 500g strong white flour 325ml tepid water (body temperature) 7/8g dried yeast sachet sprinkle of Himalayan salt good slug of extra virgin olive oil Mix the water and yeast together until the dried yeast is all wet and activated. Mix the salt and the olive oil into the flour, and make a well in the centre.

item.title: 💻 MacOS installer hung with 'Waiting for other installations to complete' 👩‍💻 🔮 🍏
item.description: For reasons I needed to test a set of osquery rules lately, so I downloaded the official package from https://osquery.io and set about running the installer. For some reason the installation got stuck at a message ‘Waiting for other installation to complete’. The installer hangs for a long time, so I explored my options to unblock the installer.

item.title: 💻 What is DevSecOps? 🔐 🧰 💻
item.description: What is DevSecOps, and how is it different to the more traditional DevOps team you are used to working with?

item.title: 💻 CRUD Put vs Patch 📝 ❌ 🧰
item.description: Should we use the http header PUT or PATCH when updating a record in a CRUD system?

item.title: 💻 Local Git init and GitHub setup 🐙 🐾 👩‍💻
item.description: A quick how-to guide to get your local git init project onto GitHub.

item.title: 💻 Naming Resources 🧮 👩‍💻 ✨
item.description: And we are back to naming things, this is my latest network device naming policy…

item.title: 💻 OpenBSD 6.9 on Raspberry Pi 4 🍓 🥧 👩‍💻
item.description: I wondered if I could use my Raspberry Pi 4 as a gateway device to shape and filter my network traffic. It only has one NIC, but OpenBSD supports many USB-NIC devices out of the box. Let’s see how successful this idea is. Update firmware on Raspberry Pi 4 Instructions here: https://github.com/AshyIsMe/openbsd-rpi4 Installing OpenBSD 6.9 on Raspberry Pi 4 Download and checksum install69.img from the OpenBSD 6.9 FTP site. Put the install69.

item.title: 💼 Resume in JSON Format 👩‍💼 📋 📎
item.description: This is worth sharing: Thomas Davies (jsonresume.org) has made a online resume host and formatter, so long as your resume is in JSON format. Let’s check it out…

item.title: 💻 Style9 Indent Rules on Linux/OpenBSD 🐧 🐡 👩‍💻
item.description: Get that lovely Plan9 source feel in your C code on Linux or OpenBSD Kernel Normal Form Kernel normal form 1, or KNF, is the coding style used in the development of code for the BSD operating systems. According to the OpenBSD man pages 2: This file specifies the preferred style for kernel source files in the OpenBSD source tree. It is also a guide for preferred userspace code style.

item.title: 💻 How To Setup A Yubikey ⛓ 🔐 ✨
item.description: A Yubikey is a physical security device for your computer (and phone if you get the NFC version). In this post we will look at adding GPG and SSH keys to the Yubikey for additional privacy and security.

item.title: 💻 Send and Receive GPG Email on MacOS ⛓ 🔐 👩‍💻
item.description: This guide is part of my series: The Ultimate Guide to GPG for Email Security. This particular page is about creating new keys for the GnuPG program on your computer.

item.title: 💻 Linux CLI Reminder 👩‍💻 📺 🐧
item.description: I find myself Google-ing for command-line fu a lot more than I should, here are some favourites… rsync with ssh Although this should be the default by now, really, who trusts the defaults? Manage it yourself with something like this: rsync -avP -e "ssh -i ssh_key_file -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null" /home/jas/local-dir/* remote-user@webhost.com:/home/server/www/yourdomain.com/ Rainbow Shell Prompt Put this in .bashrc or wherever is appropriate for your OS: export PS1="\[$(tput bold)\]\[$(tput setaf 1)\]\u\[$(tput setaf 208)\]@\[$(tput setaf 3)\h\[$(tput setaf 2)\]:\d \[$(tput setaf 4)\]\@:\[$(tput setaf 55)\]\w\n$PROMPT_COLOR\$ \[$(tput sgr0)\]" YouTube-dl on Linux Obtain it from here: https://github.

item.title: 💻 Exchanging GPG Public Keys ⛓ 🔐 👩‍💻
item.description: This guide is part of my series: The Ultimate Guide to GPG for Email Security. This particular page is about creating new keys for the GnuPG program on your computer.

item.title: 💻 Ramdisk (tmpfs) for MacOS 💾 👩‍💻 🍏
item.description: This post contains a cute script to mount and unmount a ram disk device on MacOS, and also a couple of notes about wear leveling on SSD and how this script is different to tmpfs from Unix/Solaris.

item.title: 💻 Create GnuPG Master Keys and Subkeys ⛓ 🔐 👩‍💻
item.description: This guide is part of my series: The Ultimate Guide to GPG for Email Security. This particular page is about creating new keys for the GnuPG program on your computer.

item.title: 💻 Strip Image EXIF Data 📸 👩‍💻 🐧
item.description: Did you know that all of your images contain a bunch of metadata? This is mostly harmless. But actually some of the metadata is not harmless. Dianne Feinstein, an American, famously said, post-Snowden, that “Our courts have consistently recognized that there is no reasonable expectation of privacy in this type of metadata information and thus no search warrant is required to obtain it”. Yikes! Also, note that the EFF have thoughts on fingerprinting of digital cameras, printers, CD ROM burners, and MAC addresses in routing devices.

item.title: 💻 Installing and Configuring GPG ⛓ 🔐 👩‍💻
item.description: This guide is part of my series: The Ultimate Guide to GPG for Email Security. This particular page is about installing and configuring the GPG program on your computer.

item.title: 💻 The Ultimate Guide to GPG for Email Security ⛓ 🔐 👩‍💻
item.description: There are many guides on the web for setting up GPG keys. Most of the one I have come across are outdated, or simply incorrect. This is an attempt to right those wrongs.

item.title: 💻 Setup AWS EC2 Host for Wireguard Private VPN 🌩 🔐 👩‍💻
item.description:
(Part 1/3) In this post, I shall manually setup from scratch a Wireguard VPN on an AWS EC2 instance and make it available for secure browsing from my mobile devices.

This setup process will be full manual for our first post, to understand all the principles and terminology before Post 2 where we will use the AWS CLI and Ansible to provision the EC2 instance; and later Post 3 where we will use Terraform and Infrastructure-as-Code principles to create the whole setup automatically.

item.title: 💻 Cut & paste Emoji Codes 😍 👩‍💻 🏳‍🌈
item.description: Just dropping this here so I don’t lose it, this page has all modern emoji codes for cut & paste, e.g. if you need a computer technical lady emoji you need to do this: \U0001f469\u200D\U0001f4bb to see this: 👩‍💻 https://emojiterra.com/ FIN

item.title: 💻 Sudoku Solver (by the Prime Minister of Singapore, Hsien Loong Lee) 🌊 🧩 👩‍💻
item.description: So, in 2015, the Prime Minister of Singapore, Hsien Loong Lee, posted a C language sudoku solver to leetcode.com and I can see a lot of bitwise manipulation in the code. I think it is worth our time to take the solution to pieces and see how it runs so fast.

item.title: 💻 The Thing Namer 🐍 👩‍💻 ✨
item.description:
There are two hard problems in computer science: we have one joke, and it’s not funny.

– heyjdp

I have things that need naming. So I propose a short project to arbitrarily name projects, because why not? It’s fun!

item.title: 💻 C Code Development in VS Code for MacOS 🌊 👩‍💻 🍏
item.description: A quick how-to guide to installing C code tooling for Microsoft VS Code on MacOS.

item.title: 💻 Python3 Development on VS Code for MacOS 🐍 👩‍💻 🍏
item.description: A quick how-to guide to installing Python3 tooling for Microsoft VS Code on MacOS.

item.title: 💻 Raspberry Pi 4 Serial Console 🍓 🥧 👩‍💻
item.description: Raspberry Pi has been through a few revisions now, and the data available on the www tends to get stale very fast. This is the right way of connecting to a Raspberry Pi 4 via a serial consoles. Which GPIO pins are used for the serial console? As can be seen in the image above and the cartoon here, the TX (PIN8) and RX (PIN10) pins for the serial port are next to the 5V power (PIN2) and GND (PIN6) pins on the top left.

item.title: 💻 Linear Feedback Shift Register ⛓ ✨ 👩‍💻
item.description: How did I get started in technology? Great question, let’s explore…

item.title: 👋 Hello, World! ✍ 👋 🗺
item.description: It is quite common to write a ‘Hello, world’ script when trying a new language, just to check the tech is working correctly, this is my contribution to the fire-hose of internet noise. You’re welcome!
